title: StatusValue: Add 'status-value:<new|modified|affected>'
body:
Closes https://github.com/storybookjs/storybook/issues/34292

<!-- If your PR is related to an issue, provide the number(s) above; if it resolves multiple issues, be sure to break them up (e.g. "closes #1000, closes #1001"). -->

<!--

Thank you for contributing to Storybook! Please submit all PRs to the `next` branch unless they are specific to the current release. Storybook maintainers cherry-pick bug and documentation fixes into the `main` branch as part of the release process, so you should not need to worry about this. For additional guidance: https://storybook.js.org/docs/contribute

-->

## What I did

Added two new `StatusValue` variants: `status-value:new`, `status-value:modified` and `status-value:affected`. These are intended for use in agentic UI review workflows to surface which stories are new or have changed content.

Changes include:

- Extended the `StatusValue` union type (`status-store/index.ts`) with `status-value:new` and `status-value:modified`
- Added SVG icon symbols (`AddIcon` for `new`, `EditIcon` for `modified`) to the sidebar `IconSymbols`
- Configured colors: `new` uses `theme.color.positive` (green), `modified` uses `theme.color.secondary` (purple)
- Inserted the new values into the status priority order between `success` and `warning`
- Updated the vitest addon's `statusValueToStoryIds` map to include the new values
- Added unit tests covering new status ranking and group aggregation behavior

## Checklist for Contributors

- [ ] Make sure this PR contains **one** of the labels below:
   <details>
     <summary>Available labels</summary>

  - `bug`: Internal changes that fixes incorrect behavior.
  - `maintenance`: User-facing maintenance tasks.
  - `dependencies`: Upgrading (sometimes downgrading) dependencies.
  - `build`: Internal-facing build tooling & test updates. Will not show up in release changelog.
  - `cleanup`: Minor cleanup style change. Will not show up in release changelog.
  - `documentation`: Documentation **only** changes. Will not show up in release changelog.
  - `feature request`: Introducing a new feature.
  - `BREAKING CHANGE`: Changes that break compatibility in some way with current major version.
  - `other`: Changes that do not fit in the above categories.

   </details>

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Added three new status types — New, Modified, Affected — with distinct icons and integrated into status ranking, sidebar, and tree views.
  * Added several sidebar stories demonstrating the new statuses and mixed/prioritized scenarios.

* **Tests**
  * Added unit tests for new status precedence and group aggregation; updated e2e expectations to reflect the label change.

* **Chores**
  * Updated UI label text from "Test status" to "Status".
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
author: valentinpalkovic

comments.author: coderabbitai
comments.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Avoid narrowing + casting here; it can break at runtime for new status values.**

With `StatusValue` expanded, `status.value as TestStatusValue` can index a missing key and crash on `.push`. This path should be safely exhaustive (or explicitly guarded).



<details>
<summary>🛠️ Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
 type TestStatusValue = Extract<
   StatusValue,
-  `status-value:${'pending' | 'success' | 'error' | 'warning' | 'unknown'}`
+  | `status-value:${'pending' | 'success' | 'error' | 'warning' | 'unknown'}`
+  | `status-value:${'new' | 'modified' | 'affected'}`
 >;
```

```diff
   const statusValueToStoryIdsMap: StatusValueToStoryIds = {
     'status-value:pending': [],
     'status-value:success': [],
+    'status-value:new': [],
+    'status-value:modified': [],
+    'status-value:affected': [],
     'status-value:error': [],
     'status-value:warning': [],
     'status-value:unknown': [],
   };
```

```diff
-    statusValueToStoryIdsMap[status.value as TestStatusValue].push(status.storyId);
+    statusValueToStoryIdsMap[status.value].push(status.storyId);
```
</details>


Also applies to: 51-51

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@code/addons/vitest/src/use-test-provider-state.ts` around lines 23 - 29, The
current narrowing to TestStatusValue and casting (e.g., using status.value as
TestStatusValue to index StatusValueToStoryIds) can crash if new StatusValue
variants appear; update the code to avoid unsafe casts by making the mapping
creation and access exhaustive or guarded: initialize StatusValueToStoryIds with
all possible StatusValue keys (or use a Map keyed by StatusValue) and replace
direct casts like status.value as TestStatusValue with a safe check/lookup
(e.g., verify the key exists or use a default bucket) before calling .push;
adjust functions that reference TestStatusValue and StatusValueToStoryIds so
they either compute keys from StatusValue without casting or handle unknown
values explicitly.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:hawk -->

comments.author: coderabbitai
comments.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Use distinct semantic colors for `new`, `modified`, and `affected`.**

These three values are currently collapsed to `theme.fgColor.accent`, which removes status distinction and diverges from the status rendering mapping. They should stay color-specific.



<details>
<summary>🎯 Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
-      'status-value:new': theme.fgColor.accent,
-      'status-value:modified': theme.fgColor.accent,
-      'status-value:affected': theme.fgColor.accent,
+      'status-value:new': theme.color.positive,
+      'status-value:modified': theme.color.secondary,
+      'status-value:affected': theme.color.warning,
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
      'status-value:new': theme.color.positive,
      'status-value:modified': theme.color.secondary,
      'status-value:affected': theme.color.warning,
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@code/core/src/manager/components/sidebar/StatusButton.tsx` around lines 21 -
23, The three status color entries in the StatusButton component are all set to
theme.fgColor.accent, losing semantic distinction; update the mapping in
StatusButton (the 'status-value:new', 'status-value:modified',
'status-value:affected' entries) to use distinct semantic theme colors (e.g. map
'status-value:new' to theme.fgColor.success, 'status-value:modified' to
theme.fgColor.warning, and 'status-value:affected' to theme.fgColor.danger or
the equivalent names used in your theme) so each status retains its unique color
in the status rendering.
```

</details>

comments.author: coderabbitai
comments.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Fix `statusPriority` order for change-detection statuses.**

Current order makes `status-value:new` outrank `status-value:affected`. The intended order is `success < new < modified < affected < warning`.



<details>
<summary>🔁 Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
-  'status-value:modified',
-  'status-value:affected',
-  'status-value:new',
+  'status-value:new',
+  'status-value:modified',
+  'status-value:affected',
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
  'status-value:new',
  'status-value:modified',
  'status-value:affected',
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@code/core/src/manager/utils/status.tsx` around lines 32 - 34, The
statusPriority array currently places 'status-value:new' after
'status-value:affected'; update the ordering in the statusPriority constant so
it follows the intended precedence success < new < modified < affected < warning
by placing 'status-value:new' immediately after 'status-value:success', then
'status-value:modified', then 'status-value:affected', and keeping
'status-value:warning' last; modify the statusPriority definition (and any
references to it) accordingly to ensure change-detection statuses rank as:
'status-value:success', 'status-value:new', 'status-value:modified',
'status-value:affected', 'status-value:warning'.
```

</details>

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
# Description: Find all consumption sites of the changeDetection feature flag

# Search for references to changeDetection in the codebase
rg -n -C3 -P 'features\??\.\s*changeDetection' --type ts --type tsx
```

---

```shell
#!/bin/bash

rg -n -C3 'changeDetection' --type ts
```

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
# Check the structure of common-preset.ts and how it defines features
cat -n code/core/src/core-server/presets/common-preset.ts | head -250
```

Repository: storybookjs/storybook

Length of output: 10401

```shell
rg -n 'features\?\.' code/ --type ts -A 2 | head -100
```

Length of output: 2983

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
# Get the full context around the feature flag definition
sed -n '530,560p' code/core/src/types/modules/core-common.ts
```

Repository: storybookjs/storybook

Length of output: 928

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell

# Search for TODO or FIXME related to changeDetection or agentic
rg -n -i 'todo|fixme|wip' code/core/src/types/modules/core-common.ts -B 5 -A 5
```

Length of output: 1476

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell

# Search for "agentic" references that might provide context
rg -n 'agentic' --type ts
```

Repository: storybookjs/storybook

Length of output: 165

---



</details>

**Documentation claims `@default true` but no runtime default exists.**

The JSDoc indicates this flag defaults to `true`, but the default features preset at `code/core/src/core-server/presets/common-preset.ts:205-220` does not include `changeDetection`. Since consumption sites use optional chaining (e.g., `features?.changeDetection`), an undefined value is treated as falsy, making the effective default `false`, not `true`.

This mismatch will mislead users who expect the feature to be enabled by default.

**Choose one of the following fixes:**

<details>
<summary>Option 1: Remove the misleading `@default true` comment (if feature should be opt-in)</summary>

```diff
 /**
  * Enable change detection for agentic UI review.
- *
- * `@default` true
  */
 changeDetection?: boolean;
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>Option 2: Add the default to the preset (if feature should default to enabled)</summary>

In `code/core/src/core-server/presets/common-preset.ts`, add:

```typescript
export const features: PresetProperty<'features'> = async (existing) => ({
  ...existing,
  argTypeTargetsV7: true,
  legacyDecoratorFileOrder: false,
  disallowImplicitActionsInRenderV8: true,
  viewport: true,
  highlight: true,
  controls: true,
  interactions: true,
  actions: true,
  backgrounds: true,
  outline: true,
  measure: true,
  sidebarOnboardingChecklist: true,
  componentsManifest: true,
+ changeDetection: true,
});
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@code/core/src/types/modules/core-common.ts` around lines 539 - 544, The JSDoc
for the optional flag changeDetection claims "@default true" but the features
preset does not set it, causing an effective default of false; to fix, update
the features PresetProperty<'features'> export (the features variable in the
common preset) to include changeDetection: true so runtime matches the
documented default, and verify the changeDetection?: boolean JSDoc remains
accurate.
```

</details>

comments.author: valentinpalkovic
comments.body: @MichaelArestad suggested to use `@storybook/icons` here instead. This will be a follow up task, as soon as the icons are indeed available.

comments.author: ghengeveld
comments.body: IIRC it's not possible to load `@storybook/icons` icons as symbols due to the way they're exported (as svgs rather than symbols). The symbol trick is pretty relevant for performance, otherwise we'll end up rendering hundreds of unique SVGs in the sidebar.
